anthropic.note: Enforced via daily lockfile /tmp/audit_check_ai_last_run.txt — at most one audit fire per UTC day. Each fire = 1 Opus extended-thinking call (~$2). No Haiku callers in active code; if reintroduced, pair with explicit enforcement and update this cap.

gemini.note: Hard-coded in gemma4_helper.py _GEMINI_DAILY_LIMIT (added 2026-04-25). Currently only audit_check + audit_fire_debate use it; cap has plenty of headroom.

ai_consensus.gemini:
**Reasoning:** 0 projected calls yet 26 timers implies dynamic triggers evading static analysis. Application-level caps (`/tmp/`, memory) reset on container restarts. 

**a) Runaway loops:** Crash-looping containers wipe the `/tmp/` Anthropic lockfile and Gemini memory variables, causing infinite API calls on reboot.
**b) Missing caps:** No provider-level or API-gateway billing limits exist. Horizontal scaling or multi-threading bypasses all localized caps.
**c) 2x cost blow-up:** Gemini doubling is a minor leak. Anthropic doubling locally is $4/day, but a 2x scale-out bug instantly multiplies Opus costs.
**d) Silent burner:** Anthropic. Opus extended-thinking silently burns ~$2 per crash-reboot cycle.

**Forecast if wrong:** If `/tmp/` actually maps to persistent storage, Anthropic won't
ai_consensus.anthropic:
**Audit verdict: "LOW" is misleading — projected=0 means discovery failed, not that risk is zero.**

(a) **Runaway loops:** 26 timers found but zero calls projected → the scanner isn't resolving call sites. Any timer that re-fires on exception (retry-on-error without backoff) can loop unbounded; Opus at ~$2/call hits triple digits in an hour.

(b) **Missing caps:** Anthropic cap relies on a `/tmp` lockfile — wiped on reboot/container restart, so "1/day" silently resets to N/day. No Haiku cap if reintroduced. Gemini cap is per-process, not global.

(c) **2× rate:** Opus audit at 2/day = ~$120/mo; if lockfile fails and timer fires hourly, ~$1.4k/day.

(d) **Most likely silent burner:** `audit_fire_debate` — debate loops multiply Opus calls per fire, no per-call ceiling visible.
ai_consensus.grok:
**Anthropic is the primary blow-up vector.**  
The single daily lockfile cap ($2 Opus call) has no runtime guard; concurrent timers (26 discovered), deleted /tmp file, or multi-process races bypass it instantly → runaway loop burns $50–200/day unseen.  

Gemini’s 100/day hard cap has headroom but zero per-caller metering; if audit_fire_debate doubles, costs stay hidden.  

**Most likely silent burner:** Anthropic. Zero projected calls today means operators will ignore it until the first surprise $100+ invoice. Add process-level locking + daily spend log before go-live.
